Fantastic Mr. Fox (2009)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $21,002,919Details
International Box Office $24,929,302
Worldwide Box Office $45,932,221
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $16,181,157
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,923,293
Total $20,104,450
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After 12 years of bucolic bliss, Mr. Fox breaks a promise to his wife and raids the farms of their human neighbors, Boggis, Bunce and Bean. Giving in to his animal instincts endangers not only his marriage but also the lives of his family and their animal friends. When the farmers force Mr. Fox and company deep underground, he has to resort to his natural craftiness to rise above the opposition.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$265,900 (1.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.02 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:45.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/2,034 max. theaters, 3.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $33,344,492

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 13th, 2009 (Limited) by 20th Century Fox
November 25th, 2009 (Expands Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: October 23rd, 2009 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
December 24th, 2009 (Wide) (South Korea)
January 7th, 2010 (Wide), released as The Fantastic Mr. Fox (New Zealand)
January 28th, 2021 (Limited) (Australia)
July 13th, 2023 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: March 23rd, 2010 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG For action, smoking and slang humor
Running Time: 88 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead
Plot point: Food, Heist, Mid-Life Crisis, Talking Animals
Social setting: Life on a Farm
Subgenre: Crime Comedy
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Stop-Motion Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: American Empirical, 20th Century Fox, Regency Enterprises
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

After 12 years of bucolic bliss, Mr. Fox breaks a promise to his wife and raids the farms of their human neighbors, Boggis, Bunce and Bean. Giving in to his animal instincts endangers not only his marriage but also the lives of his family and their animal friends. When the farmers force Mr. Fox and company deep underground, he has to resort to his natural craftiness to rise above the opposition.
